join_order.rationale: Nation is trivially small (25 rows); supplier keys are dense 1-100K, enabling O(1) flat array lookup

join_order.build: part(2M) filtered by p_name LIKE '%green%' → ~108K rows → bitset(2M bits)
join_order.rationale: Part filter reduces 2M to ~108K (5.4% selectivity); bitset is O(1) membership; partsupp reduces 8M to ~432K (each green part has 4 suppliers)

join_order.output: per-row: nation_str, year, amount = l_extendedprice*(1-l_discount) - ps_supplycost*l_quantity
join_order.rationale: Orders has no single-table filter; build once, probe 60M lineitem rows. Lineitem is always the probe side (largest table)

join_order.build: lineitem probe pipeline: for each l row, probe partsupp_map({l_partkey,l_suppkey}), probe supplier_flat[l_suppkey], probe orders_map[l_orderkey]
join_order.rationale: Single-pass fused scan over lineitem avoids rematerialization; all probes are O(1) lookups

logical_plan.aggregation_detail: ~175 groups (25 nations × 7 years 1992-1998); use flat 2D array agg[nation_idx][year-1992] += amount; thread-local arrays merged after parallel scan

filter_pushdown.technique: substring scan on dictionary strings; the p_name dictionary is small (~1.8M distinct but string scan on 2M rows with SIMD strstr)

logical_plan.year_extraction: O(1) lookup table YEAR_TABLE[epoch_day] from date_utils.h — applied to o_orderdate after orders hash probe

data_structures.nation_lookup: direct_array int16_t nation_dict_id[26] indexed by n_nationkey (25 entries, trivial)
data_structures.supplier_to_nation: flat_array int16_t supplier_nation[100001] indexed by s_suppkey (dense 1-100K, cache-friendly O(1))
data_structures.part_filter: bitset(2000001 bits = 250KB) on p_partkey — set bit for each green part; fits in L3 cache
data_structures.partsupp_join: compact_hash_map<uint64_t composite_key, int64_t ps_supplycost> pre_sized(432K * 1.5 = 648K slots, open-addressing robin-hood)
data_structures.orders_join: compact_hash_map<int32_t o_orderkey, int32_t o_orderdate> pre_sized(15M * 1.5 = 22.5M slots, partitioned build across 64 threads to avoid contention)
data_structures.aggregation: thread_local flat_array double agg[25][8] (nation_idx × (year-1992)); merge after parallel phase; 200 slots per thread = ~12KB total per thread

build_phases.synchronization: partitioned_no_contention (partition by high bits of composite key, 64 partitions)
partitioned_build_64_partitions (each thread owns its partitions; final merge into single map)

per_row_operations: 1. probe partsupp_map({l_partkey, l_suppkey}) → ps_supplycost (skip row if miss)
probe_phase.filter_chain: partsupp probe acts as primary filter (~432K/60M ≈ 0.72% hit rate on lineitem); majority of rows exit at step 1

parallelism.build_parallelism: partitioned_no_contention for partsupp and orders builds
parallelism.probe_parallelism: independent morsels on lineitem; all hash structures read-only during probe

index_usage.rationale: part needs full scan for LIKE '%green%' substring match; no index can accelerate this

index_usage.purpose: considered but not used — building in-memory hash map from scanned partsupp is faster than random-access index I/O for 8M rows

physical_plan.memory_pattern: streaming_sequential for all table scans; random_access for hash probes (orders 15M map may cause cache misses — mitigated by 376GB RAM fitting entire working set)

output_phase.merge: Sum thread-local agg[25][8] arrays into global result (sequential, trivial for 200 slots)
output_phase.sort: ORDER BY nation ASC, o_year DESC — sort 175 result rows (trivial std::sort)

estimated_cost.target_vs_umbra: Umbra=200ms → target <200ms with 64-thread parallelism and specialized flat structures
